DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. THOMAS V. CHRISTOPHER, TEL. NO. 1-415-659-1805 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-COMPLAINANT;
ATTY. JULIE A. STOCKTON OF KING & SPALDING, TEL. NO. 1-650-422-6818 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS DEFENDANTS LOVELY AND RENTPATH.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANTS DISCOVERY HOME NETWORK, INC. DBA LOVELY and RENTPATH, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT OF 3 TAPS, INC. - Cross-defendants Lovely and Rentpath's demurrer to both claims alleged in the first amended cross-complaint of 3taps is overruled as to both claims. The first cause of action adequately alleges a claim for breach of both an oral and written contract against Lovely. The second cause of action adequately alleges a claim for tortious interference with a written contract by Rentpath. Most or all of the grounds asserted for the demurrer are either based on assertions contrary to what is alleged in the first amended cross-complaint or require factual support that is not appropriate on a demurrer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-09-2015 Continued To Jan-13-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attys. GEORGE MORRIS, ESQ. (Tel No. 650-422-6718) and JULIE A. STOCKTON, ESQ. (Tel No. 650-422-6818) of King & Spalding LLP appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. THOMAS V. CHRISTOPHER, ESQ. of the Law Offices of Thomas V. Christopher, Tel No. 415-659-1805 appearing for Defendants Robert Kidd and 3 Taps Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS ROBERT KIDD AND 3 TAPS INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, Defendants' demurrer is OVERRULED. Plaintiff made sufficient allegations to avoid a demurrer. There is a split of authority whether the alter ego doctrine must be pleaded in the complaint. (Leek v. Cooper (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 399, 413.) "Some cases say that it must be, while others state that if a defendant is charged with liability, the denial of liability is sufficient to place the alter ego doctrine at issue." (Ibid.) Plaintiff sufficiently alleges the ultimate facts required for alter ego, i.e., domination and control, unity of interest and ownership, undercapitalization, failure to abide by corporate formalities, commingling of assets and risk of injustice. (Rutherford Holdings, LLC v. Plaza Del Rey (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 221, 235.) Plaintiff does not need to plead fraud to state an alter ego claim. (Robbins v. Blecher (1997) 52 Cal.App.4th 886, 892.) Plaintiff's claim for breach of contract is proper because Craigslist's complaint falls within the ambit of the indemnity agreement. Plaintiff's claim for promissory estoppel sets forth a prima facie claim for detrimental reliance. Defendants are unpersuasive in contending that equitable indemnity cannot be pled in the alternative where there exists a contractual indemnity claim. (See E. L. White, Inc. v. City of Huntington Beach (1978) 21 Cal.3d 497, 508 ["[w]hen, however, the duty established by contract is by the terms and conditions of its creation inapplicable to the particular factual setting before the court, the equitable principles of implied indemnity may come into play."] ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
